title: Money settled, like silt in a calm pond
subtitle: A short visualisation for building the felt sense of money resting undisturbed.
excerpt: A five-minute visualisation for when money feels unstable rather than settled. The image: silt that has drifted to the bottom of a still pond, undisturbed, sitting there.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. Creative visualisation as a self-development tool draws on public-domain traditions.

content.text: A five-minute visualisation for building the felt sense of money that has settled and stayed. The image is small and specific: silt at the bottom of a pond.
Sit or lie down. Close your eyes. One breath in, one out slowly.

Picture a pond, small, still, clear. No wind. The surface is flat. At the bottom, fine silt rests against the clay: particles that have drifted down over a long time and settled there. Nothing is disturbing them. They are simply where they are.

Let that image stand in for your money. It has settled. It is at the bottom, not rushing anywhere, not about to be stirred. Normal weather passes overhead. The surface ripples and goes still again. The silt does not move.
Hold the image for two minutes. Let your breathing slow to match the stillness of the water. Then open your eyes.
Original to homemade.education. The image method has long been used in self-help. This one uses it for the feeling of money settling and staying.
